sdo:itemDesc.dc:title: Handwritten letter from Oppel to Dr. Schmitt, dated May 3, 1933
sdo:itemDesc.dc:description: Oppel lists some of his compositions by category, including publishers' names, performers and performances. -- He offers to send copies of reviews. -- He mentions his work as chair of a cell of the Nazi Party investigating the history of the Leipzig Conservatory.

tei:opener.tei:salute: Mein lieber Herr Dr. Schmitt,Dr. Schmitt: this person has not been identified.

tei:div.tei:p: wie mir mein Freund Knettel aus Bingen schreibt, wünschen Sie Auskunft über meine Kompositionen, deren Aufführungen und Erfolge. Hier ist ein Teil der Liste.
Männerchöre bei Kistner erschienen, meist von Lehrergesangvereinen aufgeführt (Frankfurt, Hamburg, Düsseldorf).

Kirchenmusikalisches bei Vandenhoeck u Ruprecht in Göttingen.Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht: scholarly publishing house founded in Göttingen in 1735, not known as a major music publisher. Von Violinsonaten ist nur gedruckt Solosonate op 12 in d-moll bei Tischer u. Jagenberg,There is also a Suite for solo violin in E minor, Op. 19, a Duet for two violins in A minor, Op. 22, and a Sonata for violin and piano in G minor, Op. 7, and a Sonatina for violin and piano in D major, Op. 24. — Tischer & Jagenberg: Cologne-based publishing house, which brought out several works by Frederick Delius and Arnold Schoenberg’s Friede auf Erden Op. 13, as well as much music by Ewald Straesser (1867–1933), who taught at the Hochschule für Musik in Cologne. ein Werk, das von Hans Bassermann,Hans Bassermann (1888–1978), German violinist, who held various teaching positions in Germany until 1933, when he was dismissed from his post at the Weimar Conservatory because he was Jewish. Oppel probably met him at the Leipzig Conservatory, where he taught for two years in the late 1920s. Walter Schulze-PriscaWalter Schulze-Prisca (1879–1957), German violinist, founder and leader of the Prisca Quartet. u. andern Geigern viel gespielt wurde u. noch gespielt wird.

Von den 4 StreichquartettenString Quartets No. 2 in D minor, No. 3 in C minor, No. 4 in F minor (the key of No. 1 is not known). There is also a set of Variations and Fugue on a Theme by Bach for string quartet. ist nur das 4. in f-moll op 33 gedruckt, sehr viel gespielt. Ein Streichquintett ist Manuscript. Meine Bläserserenade für Flöte, Oboe, Klar. Fagott u. Horn ist ein von den Bläsern sehr begehrtes Stück; ein Quartett für Oboe, Violine, Viola u. Cello von 1932 ist erst zweimal aufgeführt, hier u. in Gera; es steigt demnächst im Rundfunk Mannheim.

Von Klaviersachen liegen op. 21, 26, 27 u 28Five Pieces for piano, Op. 21; Little Suite for piano, Op. 26; Four Preludes for piano, Op. 27; Chaconne in B major for piano, Op. 28. There are also Three Preludes, in F minor, G major, and F minor, and a Scherzo in D major and Tanzweise in G major, all for solo piano. gedruckt bei Breitkopf u. Härtel vor, op. 30 u 31 bei Mühlau – Kiel.Suite in E major for piano, Op. 30; Two Etudes for piano, Op. 31. — Mühlau: the publisher and principal bookseller of the University of Kiel, founded in 1902 by Walter G. Mühlau. Die Klaviersonaten (3) sind Manuscript. Die a cap. Messe hat Knettel
Knettel in Kreuznach u. Bingen gebracht. Von Orchestersachen (Suiten) ist noch nichts aufgeführt. Deutsche Musik war ja seither nichts, dagegen Stravinsky, Hindemith u. Debussy, Ravel, Schönberg, Ausland u. Juden (Schott-Verlag!!) beherrschen das Feld.

Ich habe in letzter Zeit mich wenig bemüht, meine Sachen anzubringen, da ich bei der Genoss. deutscher Tonsetzer Mitglied bin u. die Virtuosen keine Tantiemen zahlen konnten bei den heutigen Geldlagen. Vielleicht bitten Sie einmal KopatschkaCyrill Kopatschka (1909–76), German violinist, active in Darmstadt in the 1930s. – Darmstadt, dass er Ihnen die Solosonate op 12 vorspielt u. Präludium u. Var für Kl u. Violine. Er wird es gern tun für mich u. wohnt Darmstadt, Kasinostr 20II. Den besten Begriff von meiner Art erhalten Sie, wenn Sie sich das Quartett op 33 in f-moll ansehn. Kleine Partitur bei Peters. Leider kann ich Ihnen kein Ex. davon schicken, da ich selbst nur 2 bekam, die ich längst gebraucht habe. Wenn Sie noch Kritiken wünschen, so stehn solche gern zu Ihrer Verfügung. Ich suche sie dann Ende der Woche heraus. Augenblicklich habe ich als ZellenobmannZellenobmann: a term used by the National Socialist party for the chairman of a “cell,” or unit, in which a group of party members worked. des Landeskonservatoriums sehr viel zu tun, Sitzungen mit der Direction, dem Staatscommissar u. den Schülern, so dass ich vor Freitag nicht dazu komme. Wenn sie also noch Wert darauf legen, bitte ich um eine kurze Kartennachricht.

tei:address.tei:addrLine: Leipzig, May 3, 1933. W31, Könneritzstrasse 58III left side

tei:div.tei:p: My friend Knettel of Bingen writes to me to say that you would like information about my compositions, their performances and successes. Here is a partial list.
Male choruses, published by Kistner, performed mainly by teachers’ choral societies (Frankfurt, Hamburg, Düsseldorf).

Sacred musical works, published by Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Göttingen. Of my violin sonatas, only the unaccompanied Sonata in D minor, Op. 12, is published by Tischer & Jagenberg, a work that has been performed many times by Hans Bassermann, Walter Schulze-Prisca, and other violinists, and which is still performed.

Of my four string quartets, only No. 4 in F minor is published, and very often performed. A String Quintet is in manuscript. My Wind Serenade, for flute, oboe, clarinet, bassoon and horn is a piece in great demand from wind players; a Quartet from 1932 for oboe, violin, viola and cello has only been performed twice, here and in Gera; it will soon be broadcast on Mannheim Radio.

Among my works for piano, Opp. 21, 26, 27, and 28 are published by Breitkopf & Härtel, Opp. 30 and 31 by Mühlau in Kiel. My piano sonatas, of which there are three, are in manuscript. My a cappella Mass
was performed by Knettel in Kreuznach and Bingen. None of my orchestral works (suites) has yet been performed. Since then, German music has come to nought; the field is governed by Stravinsky, Hindemith and Debussy, Ravel, Schoenberg, foreigners and Jews (Schott’s publishing house!!).

Recently I have made little effort to get my works played, as I am a member of the Society of German Composers and the virtuosos cannot pay royalties under the present-day financial circumstances. Perhaps you could ask Kopatschka in Darmstadt to play my unaccompanied Sonata Op. 12 to you, and the Prelude and Variations for violin and piano. He would gladly do this for me; his address is Kasinostraße 20/II, Darmstadt. You will understand the nature of my music best by looking at the String Quartet in F minor, Op. 33; the miniature score is published by Peters. Unfortunately, I cannot send you a copy of it as I myself received only two, which I have been using for a long time. If you would also like reviews, I shall gladly make them available to you; I shall then look for them at the end of the week. At the moment I have a great deal to do as the chair of the unit at the State Conservatory: meetings with the directorship, the state commissioner and the pupils, so I cannot get to this before Friday. If you are still interested in it, please send a short reply on a postcard.
